title: calamagrostis purpurascens subsp. laricina
intro: Calamagrostis purpurascens subsp. laricina, also known as purple meadow grass or sweet grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, and its essential oil has been used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and improve mood. Calamagrostis purpurascens subsp. laricina can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and essential oils for medicinal and therapeutic purposes.
